title: Jurassic Park packed $4 million of legit 1993 computer hardware — a software engineer detailed every single piece…
excerpt: The movie featured 6 different types of computers, a PDA, a supercomputer, and more.
content_html:
Fabien Sanglard, who currently works as a Software Engineer at Google, has meticulously detailed every computer he can find in the original Jurassic Park movie, finally letting interested fans and viewers know what computers they need to get if they want to recreate the park.

The complete list and corresponding details are available on Sanglard’s website, which shows a total of six unique working computers, a PDA, two large CRT monitors, and a classic mechanical keyboard. He also covered the software that received screen time, including the meteorology software used in the movie to show the hurricane about to batter the island, which was also used in actual newsrooms at the time.

Most of the equipment listed can actually be found in the Jurassic Park Control Room, and even though most were only found in this particular set, they were all real, and most actually worked.

Sanglard also quoted the film’s special effects coordinator, Cory Faucher, from the book The Making of Jurassic Park. According to Faucher, “Everything in the set was real. We couldn't fake any of it, because audiences are so sophisticated now in their knowledge of computers. All told, $875,000 worth of computer hardware loaned by Silicon Graphics, $350,000 worth from Apple and some $500,000 in additional hardware and software went into equipping both the set and off-stage control room.”

Jurassic Park Control Room

This meant that all the gear the crew and cast were working with cost $1,725,000 back in 1992 — that’s worth more than $4 million in today’s money. The only non-working unit in the set was the Thinking Machines CM-5, which one commenter said was only the front panels of the supercomputer with red LED lights. Most of the software that we see on screen was actually working, except for the scene where Nedry was supposedly talking to his getaway driver while looking at him from a security feed on his Macintosh. Eagle-eyed viewers have long noticed that this wasn’t an actual video feed but a prerecorded video playing in QuickTime; you can even see the mouse cursor hiding the pause button.

It’s nice to see all these high-end computers that actually work the way they were meant to, unlike the nonsense that we see in many Hollywood films and TV shows that have a person (or two) typing gibberish to counter a “hacker” and then stopping the attack by just unplugging the terminal from the power source. So, if you want to see all these vintage machines in action, go ahead and watch Jurassic Park again this weekend. You can also check out this 3D-printed retro PC that looks like it's from the '80s and '90s or this M4-powered iMac G3 if you want to see more classic PCs (but running on modern hardware, this time).

Jowi Morales is a tech enthusiast with years of experience working in the industry. He’s been writing with several tech publications since 2021, where he’s been interested in tech hardware and consumer electronics.

content_html_zh:
目前在谷歌担任软件工程师的 Fabien Sanglard 详细介绍了他在原版《侏罗纪公园》电影中能找到的每台计算机，最终让感兴趣的粉丝和观众知道如果他们想要重建公园，他们需要购买哪些计算机。

完整的列表和相应的详细信息可以在 Sanglard 的网站上找到，其中总共显示了六台独特的工作计算机、一台 PDA、两台大型 CRT 显示器和一个经典的机械键盘。 他还介绍了播放时间的软件，包括电影中用来展示飓风即将袭击该岛的气象软件，该软件当时也在实际的新闻编辑室中使用。

列出的大多数设备实际上都可以在侏罗纪公园控制室中找到，尽管大多数设备只在这个特定的设备中找到，但它们都是真实的，并且大多数确实有效。

桑格拉德还引用了电影特效协调员科里·福彻（Cory Faucher）在《侏罗纪公园的制作》一书中的说法。 福彻表示：“场景中的一切都是真实的。 我们无法伪造任何内容，因为现在观众对计算机的了解非常丰富。 总而言之，Silicon Graphics 借出了价值 875,000 美元的计算机硬件，Apple 借出了价值 350,000 美元的计算机硬件，以及约 500,000 美元的额外硬件和软件，用于装备布景和舞台外的控制室。”

滑动即可水平滚动

SGI 花岗岩键盘（靛蓝风格）机械键盘约翰·雷蒙德·“雷”·阿诺德侏罗纪公园控制室

这意味着 1992 年，剧组人员和演员使用的所有设备花费了 1,725,000 美元，换算成今天的价值超过 400 万美元。 该组中唯一不工作的单元是 Thinking Machines CM-5，一位评论者称它只是带有红色 LED 灯的超级计算机的前面板。 我们在屏幕上看到的大多数软件实际上都在工作，除了 Nedry 据称正在与他的逃跑司机交谈，同时从他的 Macintosh 上的安全信息流中看着他的场景。 眼尖的观众早就注意到这不是一个真正的视频源，而是一个在 QuickTime 中播放的预先录制的视频；您甚至可以看到鼠标光标隐藏了暂停按钮。

很高兴看到所有这些高端计算机实际上按照其应有的方式工作，这与我们在许多好莱坞电影和电视节目中看到的胡言乱语不同，这些电影和电视节目中一个人（或两个）输入乱码来对抗“黑客”，然后通过拔掉终端电源来阻止攻击。 因此，如果您想观看所有这些老式机器的运转，请在本周末再次观看《侏罗纪公园》。 您还可以查看这款 3D 打印的复古 PC，它看起来像是来自 80 年代和 90 年代，或者如果您想看到更多经典 PC（但这次在现代硬件上运行），则可以查看这款 M4 驱动的 iMac G3。

Jowi Morales 是一位技术爱好者，拥有多年的行业工作经验。 自 2021 年以来，他一直在多家科技出版物上撰写文章，对科技硬件和消费电子产品很感兴趣。
